root_cause.text: The `_gdContributionsCalc` function assigns the left and right boundary variables (`iLeft` and `iRight`) to the contribution row before they are bounded to the filter window size, allowing subsequent operations to read out-of-bounds.
root_cause.evidence: The patch moves the assignments `res->ContribRow[u].Left = iLeft;` and `res->ContribRow[u].Right = iRight;` to execute after the code block that adjusts `iLeft` and `iRight` to fit within `windows_size`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix early assignment of row bounds before applying spill-off corrections.
intent_record.what: Removed the early assignment of Left and Right boundaries for the current ContribRow.
intent_record.how: Prevents storing potentially out-of-bounds or pre-adjusted edge points before the spill-off correction logic executes.

intent_record.how: Ensures the structure accurately records the calculated boundaries before calculating and storing the weights.

Fix the assignment of left and right boundaries in the contribution row structure.

relevance_review.reason: The group only contains whitespace removal and formatting changes, which do not address the out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the _gdContributionsCalc function.
The group only removes trailing whitespace from a function signature, which is a stylistic change completely unrelated to the out-of-bounds read vulnerability in _gdContributionsCalc.

The group's changes directly address the vulnerability by fixing the early assignment of the left and right boundaries before they are properly bounded, which was the root cause of the out-of-bounds reads.
The group adds a test case specifically designed to verify the fix for the out-of-bounds read vulnerability described in the root cause, making it directly related to the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: Assigns the left boundary before it is bounded to the filter window size, leading to out-of-bounds reads.
Assigns the right boundary before it is bounded to the filter window size, leading to out-of-bounds reads.

vuln_statements.explanation: The left boundary is assigned to the contribution row before it is properly bounded to the filter window size, allowing subsequent operations to read out-of-bounds.
The right boundary is assigned to the contribution row before it is properly bounded to the filter window size, allowing subsequent operations to read out-of-bounds.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:

Author: Pierre Joye <pierre.php@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 20 08:10:54 2013 +0100

    - add new files for interpolation

diff --git a/ext/gd/libgd/gd_interpolation.c b/ext/gd/libgd/gd_interpolation.c
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..29dbe19027d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ext/gd/libgd/gd_interpolation.c
@@ -0,0 +1,2590 @@
+/*
+ * Filtered Image Rescaling
+ * Based on Gems III
+ *  - Schumacher general filtered image rescaling
+ * (pp. 414-424)
+ *
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `_gdContributionsCalc` sets `res->ContribRow[u].Left = iLeft;` and `res->ContribRow[u].Right = iRight;` before performing boundary checks and potentially modifying `iLeft` and `iRight`. This exact issue is the root cause of the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit a7a53d369bfcf3208b445b9aa12aa8a6e114c5fd
Author: Pierre Joye <pierre.php@gmail.com>

    - add new files for interpolation

diff --git a/ext/gd/libgd/gd_interpolation.c b/ext/gd/libgd/gd_interpolation.c
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..29dbe19027d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ext/gd/libgd/gd_interpolation.c
@@ -0,0 +1,2590 @@
+/*
+ * Filtered Image Rescaling
+ * Based on Gems III
+ *  - Schumacher general filtered image rescaling
+ * (pp. 414-424)
+ *
bic_trace_step.reason: In `_gdContributionsCalc`, `res->ContribRow[u].Left` and `res->ContribRow[u].Right` are assigned the values of `iLeft` and `iRight` respectively, before `iLeft` and `iRight` are potentially modified (bounded) in the subsequent `if (iRight - iLeft + 1 > windows_size)` check. This matches the described vulnerability.
